paradigm: Mk.XII Phase 3a gemma family-internal alt path 1 — int8 quantized variant fallback
pod_status_final: manually_terminated_by_parent (after 13min, agent stuck in poll loop on same error)

stage_b_load_result.exception_msg: No GPU found. A GPU is needed for quantization.
stage_b_load_result.root_cause: bitsandbytes 0.43.3 pip-installed wheel compiled WITHOUT GPU support (CPU-only); torch.cuda.is_available()=False on H100 SXM 80GB pod despite nvidia-smi showing GPU present and driver 580.126.09 / CUDA 13.0

diagnostic_evidence.nvidia_smi: PASS — H100 80GB visible, 0% util, 0 MiB used
diagnostic_evidence.bnb_diagnostic_msg: The installed version of bitsandbytes was compiled without GPU support. 8-bit optimizers, 8-bit multiplication, and GPU quantization are unavailable.

H1_mfs_throughput_cap.evidence: 15GB sequential download completed successfully without platform termination. v1/v2/v3 fp16 attempts (54GB) all silently terminated at 12-75% download. Cross-comparison suggests download size threshold somewhere between 15GB (PASS) and 54GB (FAIL). H1 mfs throughput cap as continuous-rate constraint REJECTED; H1 as cumulative-bytes-or-time threshold STILL_CANDIDATE.
H1_mfs_throughput_cap.implication: alt path 2 (persistent volume, pre-download via dedicated pod) likely viable; alt path 4 (offline rsync) likely viable

H4_runpod_orchestrator_termination.verdict: REFINED — termination triggers on cumulative-bytes-downloaded threshold, NOT idle detection
H4_runpod_orchestrator_termination.evidence: 15GB completed download (5min sustained) + pod still alive after Stage A; previous v1/v2/v3 terminated at progressive download depths (12%/75%/12% of 54GB = 7-40GB cumulative). Threshold inferred ~30-40GB before termination.

frozen_hypothesis_verdicts_status.HMK-A_GEMMA_SCALE_INVARIANT: INCONCLUSIVE_PERSISTENT (3-cell scale curve still incomplete; int8 measurement was not obtained)

Cost: ~$8/month volume + one-time download cost.

own_4_compliance.step_b_canonical_helper_fix: PARTIAL — wrapper exception handler captured FAIL cleanly with structured summary.json (defensive harness validated); fix for retry: wrapper must pip-install bnb from CUDA-aware source OR use pre-built bnb-cuda wheel OR validate torch.cuda.is_available() before attempting bnb load

raw_10_caveats: int8 measurement was NOT obtained — Stage A (download) PASS but Stage B (load) FAIL on bnb compatibility
H1 hypothesis WEAKENED but not REFUTED — 15GB PASS vs 54GB FAIL data point gives threshold ~30-40GB but does not confirm cumulative-bytes-threshold mechanism

agent #17 polling loop did not auto-recover from bnb error (no retry attempt with cuda-aware bnb install in wrapper); manual intervention required, suggesting wrapper template needs `try-bnb-then-fallback-to-fp16-with-different-loader` resilience pattern

pod region for int8 attempt (memInGb=287, IP 87.120.211.211) was DIFFERENT from v1/v2/v3 (memInGb=251, IP 216.243.220.x) — region bias POSSIBLE confounder for H1 weakening evidence (15GB success may be region-not-size effect)

bnb 0.43.3 wheel was the latest stable as of test date; CUDA-aware install requires either (a) pip install bitsandbytes-cuda121, (b) build from source, or (c) use a pre-built docker image (e.g., nvidia/pytorch:24.x with bnb pre-installed)
